**Section 16.05 --- Amendments**

This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument executed and delivered by all Members. No amendment, modification, or supplement shall be effective unless it is in writing and signed by each of Meridian and Kastor. Any amendment, modification, or supplement effected in accordance with this Section 16.05 shall be binding upon each Member and the Company.

**Section 16.06 --- Waiver**

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement, and no consent to any departure therefrom, shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving party. No waiver shall be deemed to be, or shall constitute, a waiver of any other provision of this Agreement, whether or not similar, nor shall any waiver constitute a continuing waiver. No failure or delay on the part of any party in exercising any right, power, or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right, power, or privilege preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege.

**Section 16.07 --- Severability**

If any provision of this Agreement (or any portion thereof) is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement (or the remaining portion thereof), all of which shall remain in full force and effect. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace any provision held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable with a valid, legal, and enforceable provision that, to the greatest extent possible, achieves the original intent and economic effect of the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision.

**Section 16.08 --- Counterparts**

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Delivery of an executed counterpart of this Agreement by facsimile transmission or by email in portable document format (PDF) shall be as effective as delivery of an originally executed counterpart.

**Section 16.09 --- Confidentiality**

**(a)** Each Member shall maintain the confidentiality of all non-public information relating to the Company, the other Member, and the business and affairs of the Company and the other Member (collectively, \"**Confidential Information**\"), and shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the other Member, except as provided in this Section 16.09.

**(b)** The obligations of confidentiality set forth in this Section 16.09 shall not apply to Confidential Information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the receiving Member or its representatives in violation of this Agreement, (ii) was available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis prior to its disclosure by the disclosing Member, (iii) becomes available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing Member, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the disclosing Member, or (iv) is independently developed by the receiving Member without reference to or use of the Confidential Information.

**(c)** Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Member may disclose Confidential Information (i) to its Affiliates, directors, officers, employees, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, and other representatives who have a need to know such information in connection with the performance of such Member\'s obligations under this Agreement, provided that such Persons are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein, (ii) to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, provided that the disclosing Member provides prompt written notice to the other Member and cooperates with the other Member\'s efforts to obtain a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or (iii) to the extent necessary in connection with the enforcement of such Member\'s rights under this Agreement.

**(d)** The obligations of confidentiality set forth in this Section 16.09 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and the dissolution of the Company for a period of five (5) years.

**Section 16.10 --- Further Assurances**

Each Member shall, from time to time after the Effective Date, execute and deliver such additional instruments, documents, conveyances, or assurances and take such further actions as may be reasonably necessary or desirable to carry out the provisions of this Agreement and to effectuate the transactions contemplated hereby, including the filing of any reports, returns, or other documents with any governmental authority required in connection with the formation, organization, and operation of the Company.

**Section 16.11 --- No Third-Party Beneficiaries**

This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns, and nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other Person any legal or equitable right, benefit, remedy, or claim of any kind under or by reason of this Agreement.

**SCHEDULE 2.04**

**BUSINESS PURPOSE**

The Business Purpose of MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\") shall be to engage in the following activities:

1\. **Research and Development.** The research, development, testing, optimization, and prototyping of advanced polymer coating formulations, application technologies, curing systems, surface preparation methods, and related technologies, including both solvent-based and water-based coating systems, powder coatings, UV-curable coatings, and hybrid polymer coating systems.

2\. **Manufacturing.** The manufacture, production, compounding, blending, packaging, labeling, and quality assurance of advanced polymer coating products, including the establishment and operation of manufacturing facilities, pilot plants, and testing laboratories.

3\. **Commercialization.** The marketing, distribution, sale, licensing, and servicing of advanced polymer coating products in domestic and international markets, including the development and management of distribution channels, customer relationships, and brand identity.

4\. **Industry Verticals.** Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Company shall target the following industry verticals and application areas:

> \(a\) **Industrial Coatings.** Protective and functional coatings for industrial equipment, pipelines, storage tanks, structural steel, and other industrial infrastructure;
>
> \(b\) **Aerospace Coatings.** High-performance coatings for aircraft structures, engines, interiors, and components, including coatings meeting applicable FAA, EASA, and military specifications;
>
> \(c\) **Automotive Coatings.** Coatings for automotive OEM and aftermarket applications, including primers, basecoats, clearcoats, and specialty functional coatings;
>
> \(d\) **Marine Coatings.** Anti-fouling, anti-corrosion, and protective coatings for marine vessels, offshore structures, and port infrastructure;
>
> \(e\) **Infrastructure Coatings.** Coatings for bridges, highways, water and wastewater treatment facilities, power generation facilities, and other public and private infrastructure.

5\. **Ancillary Activities.** The performance of any and all activities ancillary, incidental, or related to the foregoing, including without limitation the procurement and management of raw materials, equipment, and supplies; the acquisition, protection, and enforcement of Intellectual Property rights; the hiring and management of employees and contractors; the entry into contracts and agreements with customers, suppliers, distributors, and other business partners; the raising of capital and incurrence of indebtedness as permitted by this Agreement; and the performance of such other lawful activities as may be approved by the Management Committee from time to time.

**SCHEDULE 3.02(b)**

**KASTOR SUPPLY-AGREEMENT COMMITMENTS**

The following sets forth the supply-agreement commitments contributed by Kastor Chemical Industries AG (\"Kastor\") to MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\"), which commitments have been valued by the Members at Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000) in the aggregate:

**1. Description of Supply-Agreement Commitments.**

Kastor commits to supply to the Company certain raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates required for the manufacture of the Company\'s advanced polymer coating products, on the terms and conditions set forth below. These commitments represent binding obligations of Kastor to provide the Company with preferential access to Kastor\'s existing supply chain, manufacturing capacity, and procurement infrastructure.

**2. Raw Materials Covered.**

The supply-agreement commitments cover the following categories of raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates:

> \(a\) Specialty epoxy resins (including bisphenol-A and bisphenol-F based resins);
>
> \(b\) Polyurethane prepolymers and isocyanate hardeners;
>
> \(c\) Fluoropolymer dispersions and PTFE micropowders;
>
> \(d\) Specialty silicone-based additives and flow control agents;
>
> \(e\) High-purity pigments and functional fillers (including titanium dioxide, zinc phosphate, and micaceous iron oxide); and
>
> \(f\) Such other raw materials as the Members may agree in writing from time to time.

**3. Volume Commitments.**

Kastor commits to supply the Company with the quantities of raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates necessary to support the Company\'s manufacturing operations during the first three (3) years following the Company\'s operational launch, up to the maximum annual volumes set forth in the detailed volume schedules to be agreed by the parties and attached hereto as an addendum prior to the Closing.

**4. Pricing.**

All raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates supplied by Kastor to the Company shall be priced at Kastor\'s internal transfer cost plus a markup not to exceed five percent (5%), which pricing shall be reviewed and, if appropriate, adjusted annually by mutual agreement of the Members. Such pricing is intended to provide the Company with cost advantages relative to prevailing market pricing for comparable raw materials.

**5. Delivery Obligations.**

Kastor shall deliver all raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates to the Company\'s manufacturing facilities (initially located at 4710 Industrial Commons Drive, Houston, Texas 77045) in accordance with delivery schedules to be mutually agreed upon by the parties. Kastor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain adequate inventory levels and to provide the Company with priority fulfillment in the event of supply constraints.

**6. Valuation.**

The aggregate value of the supply-agreement commitments described herein has been determined by the Members to be Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000), based on the estimated cost savings and preferential access provided to the Company over the initial three-year operating period relative to arm\'s-length market pricing for comparable raw materials and services.

**SCHEDULE 12.01**

**Part A --- Meridian Contributed IP**

The following sets forth the twenty-three (23) items of Intellectual Property contributed by Meridian Advanced Materials, LLC (\"Meridian\") to MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\"), including proprietary coating formulations embedded in JV products. The aggregate value of the Meridian Contributed IP is Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000) as determined by independent appraisal by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP.

  -------------- --------------------------------------------------------- ---------------------------- ----------------------------------- --------------------

  M-06           Anti-corrosion primer system for marine applications      Trade Secret / Formulation   N/A (proprietary)                   \$1,400,000

**SCHEDULE 12.02**

**CO-DEVELOPED IP**

**1. Definition and Scope.**

\"Co-Developed IP\" means all Intellectual Property developed by or on behalf of MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\") after the Effective Date (August 22, 2025) using Contributed IP (whether Meridian Contributed IP or Kastor Contributed IP), Company resources (including Company personnel, facilities, equipment, funding, and other assets), or any combination thereof. Co-Developed IP includes, without limitation, new formulations, process improvements, product designs, software, testing methodologies, technical data, know-how, and all other Intellectual Property conceived, created, developed, reduced to practice, or otherwise generated in connection with the Company\'s operations and the Business Purpose.

**2. Ownership.**

All Co-Developed IP shall be owned exclusively by the Company. Neither Member shall have any ownership interest in Co-Developed IP independent of its Membership Interest in the Company.

**3. Identification and Cataloguing.**

The Chief Technology Officer of the Company (or such other officer as the Management Committee may designate) shall be responsible for establishing and maintaining a comprehensive registry of all Co-Developed IP (the \"**Co-Developed IP Registry**\"). The Co-Developed IP Registry shall include, at a minimum, the following information for each item of Co-Developed IP:

> \(a\) A unique identification number assigned to each item;
>
> \(b\) A description of the item, including its nature (e.g., formulation, process, software, data, know-how) and the technical field to which it relates;
>
> \(c\) The date on which the item was conceived, created, or first reduced to practice;
>
> \(d\) The identity of all individuals (whether employees, contractors, or agents of the Company or any Member) who contributed to the conception, creation, or development of the item;
>
> \(e\) An identification of any Contributed IP (whether Meridian Contributed IP or Kastor Contributed IP) that was used in the development of the item;
>
> \(f\) The status of any patent applications, trademark registrations, copyright registrations, or other filings associated with the item; and
>
> \(g\) Any other information that the Management Committee may require from time to time.

**4. Reporting.**

The Chief Technology Officer shall provide the Management Committee with a quarterly report summarizing all new Co-Developed IP added to the Co-Developed IP Registry during the preceding Fiscal Quarter, together with any updates to existing entries. Such reports shall be presented at the regularly scheduled quarterly meetings of the Management Committee.

**5. Protection and Enforcement.**

The Management Committee shall determine, in its discretion, the appropriate measures to protect and enforce the Company\'s rights in Co-Developed IP, including the filing of patent applications, trademark registrations, copyright registrations, and the implementation of trade secret protection measures. All costs and expenses incurred in connection with the protection and enforcement of Co-Developed IP shall be borne by the Company.

**6. Invention Assignment.**

Each Member shall cause its employees, agents, and contractors who participate in the development of Co-Developed IP to execute invention assignment agreements in favor of the Company, in a form approved by the Management Committee, assigning all right, title, and interest in and to any Co-Developed IP to the Company.

**SCHEDULE 12.05**

**RESTRICTED COMPETITORS**

The following Persons are designated as Restricted Competitors for purposes of Articles XII and XIV of this Agreement, effective as of the Effective Date. This list is subject to update, supplementation, and modification by the Management Committee from time to time in accordance with Section 12.05 of the Agreement.

**Initial List of Restricted Competitors:**

1\. PPG Industries, Inc.

2\. The Sherwin-Williams Company

4\. Axalta Coating Systems Ltd.

5\. RPM International Inc.

6\. Hempel A/S

11\. Teknos Group Oy

12\. Carboline Company (a subsidiary of RPM International Inc.)

13\. International Paint Ltd. (a subsidiary of Akzo Nobel N.V.)

14\. Tnemec Company, Inc.

15\. Specialty Polymer Coatings, Inc.

The Management Committee may add, remove, or modify entries on this list from time to time by a majority vote of the Managers present at a duly constituted meeting of the Management Committee. Any updates to this Schedule 12.05 shall be documented in writing, distributed to each Member, and maintained with the Company\'s books and records.

*\[End of Schedules\]*

**JOINT VENTURE AGREEMENT**

This Joint Venture Agreement (this \"**Agreement**\") is made and entered into as of August 22, 2025 (the \"**Effective Date**\"), by and between:

**Meridian Advanced Materials, LLC**, a Delaware limited liability company, with its principal place of business at 900 N. Michigan Avenue, Suite 2800, Chicago, Illinois 60611 (Employer Identification Number 84-3927156) (\"**Meridian**\"); and

**Kastor Chemical Industries AG**, a corporation organized under the laws of Switzerland, with its registered office in the Canton of Zug, Switzerland (Swiss UID CHE-215.847.603) (\"**Kastor**\").

Each of Meridian and Kastor is referred to herein individually as a \"**Member**\" and collectively as the \"**Members**.\"

**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Meridian is a Delaware limited liability company (EIN 84-3927156) engaged in the research, development, and commercialization of advanced materials, including specialty polymer technologies, and possesses significant proprietary intellectual property in the field of advanced polymer coatings;

**WHEREAS**, Kastor is a Swiss corporation organized under the laws of the Canton of Zug, Switzerland (Swiss UID CHE-215.847.603), and is a leading global manufacturer and supplier of specialty chemical products, with extensive manufacturing capabilities, supply chain infrastructure, and technical expertise in polymer chemistry and industrial chemical processes;

**WHEREAS**, the Members desire to form a joint venture for the purpose of developing, manufacturing, and commercializing advanced polymer coating products by combining Meridian\'s proprietary intellectual property and technical capabilities with Kastor\'s manufacturing expertise, supply chain resources, and global market access;

**WHEREAS**, the Members have agreed to form MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"**Company**\" or the \"**JV**\"), a Delaware limited liability company, with its registered office at 1209 Kirkwood Highway, Wilmington, Delaware 19801, and its operations headquarters at 4710 Industrial Commons Drive, Houston, Texas 77045, to carry out the purposes of the joint venture as set forth herein;

**WHEREAS**, Meridian will contribute to the Company certain intellectual property consisting of twenty-three (23) items of Contributed IP (as defined herein), which intellectual property has been independently appraised by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP at a value of Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000), together with cash of Fourteen Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$14,300,000), for a total initial Capital Contribution of Forty-Two Million Nine Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$42,900,000), representing a fifty-five percent (55%) Membership Interest in the Company;

**WHEREAS**, Kastor will contribute to the Company cash of Forty-One Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$41,800,000) (being Thirty-Eight Million Euros (€38,000,000) converted at an agreed exchange rate of One Dollar and Ten Cents (\$1.10) per One Euro (€1.00)), together with supply-agreement commitments valued at Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000), for a total initial Capital Contribution of Forty-Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$43,800,000), representing a forty-five percent (45%) Membership Interest in the Company;

**WHEREAS**, the total initial capitalization of the Company shall be Eighty-Six Million Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$86,700,000);

**WHEREAS**, Capitol Statutory Services, Inc. shall serve as the registered agent of the Company in the State of Delaware, and Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP shall serve as the independent auditor of the Company;

**WHEREAS**, the Members intend to file the Certificate of Formation of the Company with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on or about September 2, 2025, with a target operational launch in the first quarter of 2026; and

**WHEREAS**, the Members desire to set forth in this Agreement their respective rights, obligations, and duties with respect to the Company, including the governance, capitalization, management, and operation of the Company and the disposition of their respective Membership Interests therein.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Members agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE I --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

As used in this Agreement, the following capitalized terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Article I shall have the meanings ascribed to them elsewhere in this Agreement.

**\"Affiliate\"** means, with respect to any Person, any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, Controls, is Controlled by, or is under common Control with, such Person. For purposes of this definition, \"**Control**\" (including the correlative terms \"**Controlled by**\" and \"**under common Control with**\") means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of a Person, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, a Person shall be deemed to Control another Person if such Person (i) owns, directly or indirectly, more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of such other Person, or (ii) has the contractual or other right to appoint a majority of the board of directors (or equivalent governing body) of such other Person. For purposes of this Agreement, the Company shall not be deemed an Affiliate of either Member.

**\"Bankrupt Member\"** means a Member that has (i) filed a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or been the subject of an involuntary petition in bankruptcy that has not been dismissed within sixty (60) days of filing, (ii) made a general assignment for the benefit of creditors, (iii) had a receiver, trustee, or custodian appointed for all or a substantial portion of its assets, or (iv) been dissolved, liquidated, or wound up (other than a voluntary dissolution in connection with a Change of Control).

**\"Business Day\"** means any day other than a Saturday, Sunday, or a day on which commercial banks in New York, New York are authorized or required by law to close.

**\"Business Purpose\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 2.04 and as more particularly described in Schedule 2.04.

**\"Capital Account\"** means the capital account maintained for each Member in accordance with Section 3.03.

**\"Capital Call\"** means a call for additional capital contributions approved by the Management Committee pursuant to Article V.

**\"Capital Call Default\"** means the failure of a Member to fund its pro-rata share of a Capital Call within the applicable Funding Deadline or, if applicable, within the Cure Period following delivery of a Default Notice, as further described in Section 5.01(d).

**\"Capital Call Default Notice\"** or **\"Default Notice\"** means the written notice of default delivered by the Company to a Defaulting Member pursuant to Section 5.01(d)(i).

**\"Capital Call Notice\"** means the written notice delivered to each Member specifying the aggregate amount required in a Capital Call and each Member\'s pro-rata share thereof, as described in Section 5.01(b).

**\"Capital Contribution\"** means the total amount of cash, property, and other consideration contributed (or deemed contributed) to the Company by a Member, including the Initial Capital Contribution and any additional Capital Contributions made pursuant to a Capital Call.

**\"Certificate of Formation\"** means the Certificate of Formation of the Company filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware, as amended from time to time.

**\"Change of Control\"** means, with respect to any Member, (i) the direct or indirect transfer, sale, assignment, or other disposition (whether in a single transaction or a series of related transactions) of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of such Member to any Person or group of Persons acting in concert who did not hold such interests immediately prior to such transaction(s), or (ii) a consolidation, merger, recapitalization, or similar transaction involving such Member in which the holders of the voting equity interests of such Member immediately prior to such transaction hold, immediately following such transaction, less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting equity interests of the surviving or resulting entity, or (iii) the sale, lease, exchange, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of such Member to any Person or group of Persons acting in concert.

**\"Closing\"** means the consummation of the Initial Capital Contributions by both Members in accordance with Section 3.02.

**\"Co-Developed IP\"** means Intellectual Property developed by or on behalf of the Company after the Effective Date using Contributed IP, Company resources, or both, as further described in Schedule 12.02.

**\"Company\"** or **\"JV\"** means MKPoly Coatings, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company formed pursuant to this Agreement.

**\"Contributed IP\"** means Intellectual Property contributed to the Company by either Member as identified in Schedule 12.01.

**\"Cure Period\"** means ninety (90) calendar days following delivery of a Capital Call Default Notice to the Defaulting Member, as described in Section 5.01(d)(ii).

**\"Defaulting Member\"** means a Member that is in Capital Call Default.

**\"Delaware LLC Act\"** means the Delaware Limited Liability Company Act, Title 6 of the Delaware Code, Chapter 18 (6 Del. C. § 18-101 et seq.), as amended from time to time.

**\"Distributable Cash\"** means, as of any date of determination, the aggregate amount of cash and cash equivalents of the Company on hand as of such date, less (i) all amounts necessary to pay or provide for the payment of all current obligations of the Company then due and payable, (ii) all amounts necessary to fund reasonably anticipated operating expenses and capital expenditures of the Company for the current and next succeeding Fiscal Quarter, and (iii) such reserves as the Management Committee may establish from time to time for contingent liabilities, working capital needs, or other purposes.

**\"EBITDA\"** means the Company\'s earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization, calculated in accordance with GAAP, for the applicable period.

**\"Effective Date\"** means August 22, 2025.

**\"Exit Event\"** means a Triggering Event leading to the dissolution, buyout, or sale of all or a portion of the Company in accordance with Article X or Article XIII.

**\"Fair Market Value\"** means the value determined in accordance with Section 10.03 by an independent appraiser selected in accordance with Section 10.03(b).

**\"Fiscal Quarter\"** means each calendar quarter ending March 31, June 30, September 30, and December 31.

**\"Fiscal Year\"** means the calendar year, commencing January 1 and ending December 31 of each year, except that the first Fiscal Year shall commence on the date of formation of the Company and end on the immediately following December 31.

**\"Funding Deadline\"** means the date that is thirty (30) calendar days after a Member\'s receipt of a Capital Call Notice, as described in Section 5.01(c).

**\"GAAP\"** means United States generally accepted accounting principles, consistently applied.

**\"Indemnified Party\"** means any Manager, officer, employee, or agent of the Company, or any Member or Affiliate thereof, who is entitled to indemnification from the Company pursuant to this Agreement or applicable law.

**\"Initial Capital Contribution\"** means the Capital Contribution made by each Member at the Closing as described in Section 3.02.

**\"Initiating Member\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 10.02(a).

**\"Intellectual Property\"** means all patents, patent applications, inventions (whether or not patentable), trade secrets, know-how, formulations, process documentation, technical data, specifications, engineering drawings, manufacturing processes, trademarks, service marks, trade names, trade dress, domain names, copyrights, copyrightable works, software, databases, and all other proprietary rights, in each case together with all registrations, applications, renewals, extensions, continuations, divisions, reissues, and equivalents thereof in any jurisdiction worldwide.

**\"Losses\"** means any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, judgments, awards, liabilities, losses, damages, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and costs of investigation), fines, penalties, and interest.

**\"Management Committee\"** means the governing body of the Company established pursuant to Section 4.01.

**\"Manager\"** means each individual appointed to serve on the Management Committee pursuant to Section 4.01.

**\"Material Breach\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 11.01.

**\"Member\"** means each of Meridian and Kastor, and their respective successors and permitted assigns, in each case for so long as such Person holds a Membership Interest. \"**Members**\" means both of them collectively.

**\"Membership Interest\"** means a Member\'s entire ownership interest in the Company, including such Member\'s economic rights (including the right to receive allocations and distributions), governance rights (including the right to vote and appoint Managers), and all other rights, benefits, and obligations attributable to such Member\'s ownership of the Company under this Agreement and applicable law.

**\"Net Profits\"** and **\"Net Losses\"** mean, for each Fiscal Year or other applicable period, the net income or net loss of the Company for such period, as determined in accordance with GAAP and adjusted in accordance with the provisions of Article VII.

**\"Non-Defaulting Member\"** means a Member that is not in Capital Call Default.

**\"Percentage Interest\"** means, with respect to each Member, the percentage of such Member\'s Membership Interest relative to the total Membership Interests of all Members. As of the Effective Date, the Percentage Interests are: Meridian --- fifty-five percent (55%); Kastor --- forty-five percent (45%). The Percentage Interests are subject to adjustment pursuant to Section 5.01(d).

**\"Permitted Transfer\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 9.02.

**\"Person\"** means any individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership, limited liability company, joint venture, trust, estate, unincorporated organization, governmental authority, or other entity of any kind.

**\"Related-Party Transaction\"** means any transaction or arrangement between the Company, on the one hand, and a Member or any Affiliate of a Member, on the other hand.

**\"Reserved Matters\"** means those matters requiring approval thresholds specified in Section 4.03(b).

**\"Restricted Business\"** means the research, development, manufacture, marketing, distribution, or sale of the specific product lines actively developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company as of the date of the applicable Member\'s exit, as identified by reference to the Company\'s then-current product catalog and revenue records.

**\"Restricted Competitors\"** means those Persons identified on Schedule 12.05, as updated from time to time by the Management Committee.

**\"Restricted Period\"** means the period of twelve (12) months following the effective date of a Member\'s exit from the Company by any means (whether through buyout, dissolution, Transfer, or otherwise).

**\"Restricted Territory\"** means the geographic territories in which the Company had active sales (defined as recorded revenues exceeding One Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$100,000) in the trailing twelve (12) months) as of the date of the applicable Member\'s exit.

**\"Selling Member\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 10.02(a).

**\"Tax Distributions\"** has the meaning set forth in Section 6.02.

**\"Tax Matters Member\"** means the Member designated to serve as the \"partnership representative\" (within the meaning of Section 6223 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended) and to manage all tax-related matters of the Company. The initial Tax Matters Member shall be Meridian.

**\"Transfer\"** means any direct or indirect sale, assignment, transfer, pledge, hypothecation, encumbrance, gift, bequest, or other disposition, whether voluntary or involuntary, by operation of law or otherwise, of all or any portion of a Member\'s Membership Interest.

**\"Triggering Event\"** means a Change of Control of either Member , a Material Breach by either Member , a Deadlock (as defined in Section 4.04) that has not been resolved through the escalation and mediation procedures set forth in Section 4.04(a) and (b), or an Involuntary Transfer (as defined in Section 9.06), as further defined in Section 10.01.

**\"Treasury Regulations\"** means the regulations promulgated under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended, by the United States Department of the Treasury, as such regulations may be amended from time to time.

**[ARTICLE II --- FORMATION AND PURPOSE]{.underline}**

**Section 2.01 --- Formation**

The Company shall be formed as a Delaware limited liability company by the filing of a Certificate of Formation with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware on or about September 2, 2025. The Certificate of Formation shall be in the form agreed upon by the Members and shall comply in all respects with the requirements of the Delaware LLC Act. Upon the filing of the Certificate of Formation, the rights, duties, and obligations of the Members shall be as provided in the Delaware LLC Act, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement. To the extent that the rights, duties, or obligations of any Member are different by reason of any provision of this Agreement than they would be under the Delaware LLC Act in the absence of such provision, this Agreement shall, to the maximum extent permitted by the Delaware LLC Act, control. The Company shall be governed by this Agreement and the Delaware LLC Act (6 Del. C. § 18-101 et seq.).

**Section 2.02 --- Name**

The name of the Company shall be \"MKPoly Coatings, LLC.\" The business of the Company shall be conducted under such name or under such other name or names as the Management Committee may from time to time determine, provided that all applicable filing and registration requirements with respect to any such name are satisfied. The Management Committee shall have the authority to change the name of the Company at any time and from time to time by amending the Certificate of Formation in accordance with the Delaware LLC Act.

**Section 2.03 --- Registered Agent and Office**

The registered agent of the Company in the State of Delaware shall be Capitol Statutory Services, Inc., and the registered office of the Company in the State of Delaware shall be at 1209 Kirkwood Highway, Wilmington, Delaware 19801. The principal office of the Company for operations shall be located at 4710 Industrial Commons Drive, Houston, Texas 77045, or at such other place as the Management Committee may from time to time designate. The Management Committee may change the registered agent, registered office, or principal office of the Company at any time and from time to time by filing appropriate amendments with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware and providing written notice thereof to the Members.

**Section 2.04 --- Purpose**

The purpose of the Company shall be to develop, manufacture, and commercialize advanced polymer coating products, including specialty polymer coatings for industrial, aerospace, automotive, marine, and infrastructure applications, as more particularly described in Schedule 2.04 attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference (the \"**Business Purpose**\"), and to engage in any and all activities ancillary or related thereto, including without limitation the following: (i) the research, development, testing, and optimization of polymer coating formulations and application technologies; (ii) the manufacture, assembly, packaging, and quality assurance of polymer coating products; (iii) the marketing, distribution, sale, licensing, and servicing of polymer coating products in the Restricted Territory and worldwide; (iv) the procurement and management of raw materials, equipment, and supplies necessary for the foregoing activities; (v) the acquisition, protection, and enforcement of Intellectual Property rights related to the foregoing activities; and (vi) the performance of such other lawful activities as may be approved by the Management Committee from time to time that are necessary, convenient, or incidental to the foregoing.

**Section 2.05 --- Term**

The Company shall continue in perpetuity unless dissolved in accordance with Article XIII of this Agreement or as otherwise required by the Delaware LLC Act. Nothing in this Section 2.05 shall limit the rights of the Members to dissolve the Company in accordance with the express provisions of this Agreement.

**Section 2.06 --- Fiscal Year**

The Fiscal Year of the Company shall be the calendar year, commencing on January 1 and ending on December 31 of each year. The first Fiscal Year of the Company shall commence on the date of formation of the Company (the date on which the Certificate of Formation is filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware) and shall end on December 31, 2025.

**[ARTICLE III --- MEMBERS; CAPITAL CONTRIBUTIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 3.01 --- Members; Percentage Interests**

The Members of the Company and their respective Percentage Interests as of the Effective Date are as follows:

  -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Member**                          **Percentage Interest**
  ----------------------------------- -----------------------------------
  Meridian Advanced Materials, LLC    55%

  Kastor Chemical Industries AG       45%

  **Total**                           **100%**
  -----------------------------------------------------------------------

The Percentage Interests set forth above are subject to adjustment solely in accordance with Section 5.01(d) of this Agreement.

**Section 3.02 --- Initial Capital Contributions**

**(a) Meridian.** At the Closing, Meridian shall contribute to the Company the following:

> \(i\) Intellectual property consisting of twenty-three (23) items of Contributed IP as set forth in Schedule 12.01 (Part A) attached hereto, including proprietary coating formulations embedded in JV products. Such Contributed IP has been independently appraised by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP at an aggregate value of Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000). The appraisal was conducted at arm\'s length in accordance with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice and standard industry methodologies for valuing intellectual property assets, and a copy of the appraisal report shall be maintained by the Company among its books and records; and
>
> \(ii\) Cash in the amount of Fourteen Million Three Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$14,300,000), to be delivered by wire transfer of immediately available funds to an account designated by the Company at the Closing.

Meridian\'s total Initial Capital Contribution shall be Forty-Two Million Nine Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$42,900,000).

**(b) Kastor.** At the Closing, Kastor shall contribute to the Company the following:

> \(i\) Cash in the amount of Forty-One Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$41,800,000), being the Dollar equivalent of Thirty-Eight Million Euros (€38,000,000) converted at an agreed fixed exchange rate of One Dollar and Ten Cents (\$1.10) per One Euro (€1.00), to be delivered by wire transfer of immediately available funds to an account designated by the Company at the Closing. For the avoidance of doubt, the exchange rate set forth herein is a fixed rate agreed upon by the Members for purposes of determining the Dollar amount of Kastor\'s cash Capital Contribution and shall not be subject to adjustment based on any change in the actual Euro-to-Dollar exchange rate at the time of funding or otherwise; and
>
> \(ii\) Supply-agreement commitments as set forth in Schedule 3.02(b) attached hereto, which commitments have been valued by the Members at Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000). Such supply-agreement commitments include raw-material supply terms, volume commitments, pricing schedules, and delivery obligations, all as more particularly described in Schedule 3.02(b).

Kastor\'s total Initial Capital Contribution shall be Forty-Three Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$43,800,000).

**(c) Total Capitalization.** The aggregate initial capitalization of the Company shall be Eighty-Six Million Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$86,700,000), consisting of the sum of Meridian\'s and Kastor\'s respective Initial Capital Contributions.

**Section 3.03 --- Capital Accounts**

A separate Capital Account shall be established and maintained for each Member in accordance with the provisions of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv) and GAAP. Each Member\'s Capital Account shall be credited with the amount of cash and the agreed fair market value (as determined in accordance with the applicable provisions of this Agreement) of any property contributed by such Member to the Company, and shall be credited with the amount of Net Profits (and items of income and gain) allocated to such Member. Each Member\'s Capital Account shall be debited with the amount of cash and the agreed fair market value of any property distributed to such Member by the Company, and shall be debited with the amount of Net Losses (and items of deduction and loss) allocated to such Member. The Capital Accounts shall be adjusted as necessary to comply with the requirements of Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv) and this Agreement.

**Section 3.04 --- No Additional Contributions**

No Member shall be required or obligated to make additional Capital Contributions to the Company except pursuant to a Capital Call approved in accordance with Article V of this Agreement. No Member shall be required to lend any funds to the Company, and no loan by a Member to the Company shall constitute a Capital Contribution or increase such Member\'s Capital Account.

**Section 3.05 --- No Interest on Contributions**

No Member shall receive any interest on any Capital Contribution to the Company, whether an Initial Capital Contribution or an additional Capital Contribution made pursuant to a Capital Call.

**Section 3.06 --- Return of Contributions**

No Member shall have the right to withdraw or demand a return of all or any portion of its Capital Contribution except upon dissolution and winding up of the Company in accordance with Article XIII or as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement. No Member shall have priority over any other Member with respect to the return of Capital Contributions, except as expressly provided in Section 13.02. Under circumstances requiring a return of any Capital Contribution, no Member shall have the right to receive property other than cash, except as may be specifically provided in this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE IV --- MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE; OFFICERS]{.underline}**

**Section 4.01 --- Establishment; Composition**

**(a) Management by Management Committee.** The business and affairs of the Company shall be managed by a Management Committee. Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or required by the Delaware LLC Act, the Management Committee shall have full, exclusive, and complete authority, power, and discretion to manage and control the business, affairs, and properties of the Company, to make all decisions regarding those matters, and to perform any and all other acts or activities customary or incident to the management of the Company\'s business.

**(b) Composition.** The Management Committee shall consist of six (6) individuals (each, a \"**Manager**\"), of whom three (3) shall be appointed by Meridian and three (3) shall be appointed by Kastor. Each Member shall designate its initial appointees to the Management Committee by written notice to the other Member and the Company prior to or at the Closing.

**(c) Removal and Replacement.** Each Member may remove and replace its appointed Managers at any time and for any reason or for no reason in its sole and absolute discretion, upon written notice to the other Member and the Company. Any vacancy on the Management Committee resulting from the death, resignation, removal, or incapacity of a Manager shall be filled by the Member that originally appointed such Manager. No Manager may be removed by any Person other than the Member that appointed such Manager.

**(d) No Authority of Individual Managers.** No individual Manager shall have the authority to bind the Company or take any action on behalf of the Company, except as expressly delegated by the Management Committee or as otherwise provided in this Agreement.

**Section 4.02 --- Meetings; Quorum**

**(a) Regular and Special Meetings.** The Management Committee shall hold regular meetings at least quarterly, at such times and places as the Management Committee shall determine. Special meetings of the Management Committee may be called by any Manager upon delivery of written notice to all other Managers not less than ten (10) Business Days prior to the date of such meeting. Any notice of a special meeting shall specify the purpose or purposes of the meeting and shall be accompanied by such supporting materials as may be reasonably necessary to enable the Managers to prepare for the matters to be considered at such meeting. Meetings may be held in person, by telephone conference, by videoconference, or by any other means of communication that permits all participating Managers to communicate with each other simultaneously during the meeting.

**(b) Quorum.** A quorum for the transaction of business at any meeting of the Management Committee shall consist of five (5) Managers, of whom at least two (2) must be a Kastor appointee and at least two (2) must be Meridian appointees. No business shall be transacted at any meeting of the Management Committee unless a quorum is present. If a quorum is not present at any duly called meeting, the Managers present may adjourn such meeting to a later date not less than five (5) nor more than fifteen (15) Business Days thereafter, upon written notice to all Managers, and if a quorum is present at such adjourned meeting, the Management Committee may transact any business that could have been transacted at the originally scheduled meeting.

**(c) Action by Written Consent.** The Management Committee may act by written consent in lieu of a meeting if such written consent is signed by the number of Managers that would be required to approve the action at a duly constituted meeting at which all Managers were present. Any such written consent shall be filed with the minutes of the Management Committee and shall have the same force and effect as a vote at a meeting.

**Section 4.03 --- Voting; Reserved Matters**

**(a) Ordinary Matters.** Each Manager shall have one (1) vote on each matter submitted to the Management Committee for its approval. Except as set forth in Section 4.03(b) below, all actions of the Management Committee shall be decided by a simple majority vote of the Managers present at a duly constituted meeting at which a quorum is present.

**(b) Reserved Matters.** The following matters (the \"**Reserved Matters**\") shall require the affirmative vote of at least five (5) of the six (6) Managers (a \"Supermajority Vote\"):

> \(i\) Approval of the annual business plan and operating budget of the Company for each Fiscal Year, including any material amendments or modifications thereto;
>
> \(ii\) Authorization of capital expenditures exceeding Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000) individually or Five Million Dollars (\$5,000,000) in the aggregate in any Fiscal Year, in each case that are not contemplated by the approved annual operating budget;
>
> \(iii\) Incurrence of indebtedness (including the issuance of guarantees or the creation of liens on Company assets to secure indebtedness) in an amount exceeding Five Million Dollars (\$5,000,000), whether in a single transaction or in a series of related transactions;
>
> \(iv\) Any acquisition, disposition, lease, or encumbrance of assets of the Company with a value exceeding Three Million Dollars (\$3,000,000), whether in a single transaction or in a series of related transactions, that is not contemplated by the approved annual operating budget;
>
> \(v\) Issuance of additional Membership Interests or admission of new Members to the Company;
>
> \(vi\) Approval of Capital Calls pursuant to Article V;
>
> \(vii\) Material amendments to employee benefit plans or executive compensation arrangements of the Company, including any equity-based or profit-sharing compensation plans;
>
> \(viii\) Commencement or settlement of any litigation, arbitration, or other legal proceeding involving claims exceeding One Million Dollars (\$1,000,000);
>
> \(ix\) Entry into, material amendment of, or termination of any Related-Party Transaction;
>
> \(x\) Amendment of this Agreement pursuant to Section 16.05;
>
> \(xi\) Dissolution of the Company pursuant to Section 13.01(a).

**Section 4.04 --- Deadlock Resolution**

In the event that the Management Committee is unable to reach a decision on any matter after two (2) consecutive duly noticed meetings at which such matter is considered and voted upon (a \"**Deadlock**\"), the following procedure shall apply:

**(a) Escalation.** The matter shall be referred to the respective chief executive officers (or equivalent senior executives) of Meridian and Kastor for good-faith negotiations for a period of thirty (30) calendar days (the \"**Escalation Period**\").

**(b) Mediation.** If the matter remains unresolved at the end of the Escalation Period, the parties shall submit the matter to non-binding mediation administered by the American Arbitration Association in Wilmington, Delaware, before a single mediator with experience in the specialty chemicals industry. The mediation shall be completed within sixty (60) calendar days of its initiation.

**(c) Forced Buyout.** If the Deadlock is not resolved through mediation, either Member may, by written notice to the other Member within thirty (30) calendar days following the conclusion of the mediation (the \"**Buyout Election Notice**\"), elect to initiate the buyout procedure set forth in Article X, and such Deadlock shall constitute a Triggering Event for purposes of Article X. If both Members deliver a Buyout Election Notice, the Member with the higher Percentage Interest shall be deemed the Initiating Member; if Percentage Interests are equal, the Member who first delivered a Buyout Election Notice shall be deemed the Initiating Member.

**Section 4.05 --- Officers**

**(a) Appointment of Officers.** The Management Committee shall appoint a Chief Executive Officer (\"**CEO**\"), a Chief Financial Officer (\"**CFO**\"), a Chief Technology Officer (\"**CTO**\"), and such other officers as the Management Committee may deem appropriate from time to time. Officers shall have such powers and duties as may be delegated to them by the Management Committee, subject to the limitations set forth in this Agreement.

**(b) Initial Officers.** The initial CEO of the Company shall be appointed by Meridian. The initial CTO of the Company shall be appointed by Kastor. The initial CFO of the Company shall be jointly approved by the Members.

**(c) Term of Office.** Each officer of the Company shall serve at the pleasure of the Management Committee and may be removed by the Management Committee at any time, with or without cause, by the affirmative vote of a majority of the Managers. Any vacancy in any office may be filled by the Management Committee in accordance with this Section 4.05.

**(d) Duties and Responsibilities.** The CEO shall have general supervision and management of the business and affairs of the Company and shall report to the Management Committee. The CFO shall be responsible for the financial management and reporting of the Company, including the preparation and maintenance of the Company\'s books and records, financial statements, and tax returns. The CTO shall be responsible for the technical direction and oversight of the Company\'s research, development, and manufacturing activities. Each officer shall perform such additional duties as may be assigned from time to time by the Management Committee.

**Section 4.06 --- Compensation of Managers**

Managers shall not receive any compensation from the Company for their service as Managers on the Management Committee. Each Manager shall, however, be entitled to reimbursement from the Company for reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses actually incurred by such Manager in connection with attending meetings of the Management Committee and performing duties on behalf of the Company, including travel, lodging, and meal expenses. Requests for reimbursement shall be submitted in accordance with the Company\'s expense reimbursement policies as established by the Management Committee from time to time.

**Section 4.07 --- Standard of Care; Exculpation**

**(a) Standard of Care.** Each Manager shall discharge such Manager\'s duties as a Manager of the Company in good faith, with the care an ordinarily prudent person in a like position would exercise under similar circumstances, and in a manner such Manager reasonably believes to be in the best interests of the Company. A Manager shall be fully protected in relying in good faith upon the records of the Company and upon information, opinions, reports, or statements presented to the Management Committee by any officer or employee of the Company, or by any accountant, appraiser, legal counsel, or other professional or expert selected with reasonable care by the Company or the Management Committee.

**(b) Exculpation.** No Manager shall be personally liable to the Company, to any Member, or to any other Person for monetary damages for breach of fiduciary duty or other duty as a Manager, except that this Section 4.07(b) shall not eliminate or limit the liability of a Manager for (i) acts or omissions constituting bad faith, willful misconduct, or fraud, (ii) any transaction from which such Manager derived an improper personal benefit, or (iii) any act or omission for which liability may not be eliminated or limited under the Delaware LLC Act as in effect at the time of such act or omission.

**(c) Indemnification.** The Company shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless each Manager and each officer of the Company (each, an \"**Indemnified Party**\") from and against any and all Losses incurred by such Indemnified Party by reason of such Person\'s service as a Manager, officer, employee, or agent of the Company, to the fullest extent permitted by the Delaware LLC Act, provided that no indemnification shall be available with respect to any matter as to which such Indemnified Party has been adjudicated to have engaged in bad faith, willful misconduct, or fraud. The Company shall advance expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) incurred by an Indemnified Party in defending any claim, action, or proceeding for which indemnification may be sought hereunder, upon receipt of an undertaking by or on behalf of such Indemnified Party to repay such amounts if it is ultimately determined that such Indemnified Party is not entitled to indemnification hereunder.

**[ARTICLE V --- CAPITAL CALLS; FAILURE TO FUND]{.underline}**

**Section 5.01 --- Capital Calls**

**(a) Approval.** If at any time after the Closing the Management Committee determines that the Company requires additional capital beyond the Initial Capital Contributions and any available internal resources, the Management Committee may approve a Capital Call in accordance with Section 4.03(b). Any Capital Call shall require the affirmative vote of at least five (5) of the six (6) Managers (a Supermajority Vote), as a Reserved Matter.

**(b) Notice.** Upon approval of a Capital Call by the Management Committee, the Company shall promptly deliver to each Member a written Capital Call Notice specifying (i) the aggregate amount of additional capital required, (ii) each Member\'s pro-rata share of such Capital Call (based on such Member\'s then-current Percentage Interest), (iii) the purpose for which the additional capital is required, and (iv) the Funding Deadline by which each Member\'s contribution must be received.

**(c) Funding.** Each Member shall fund its pro-rata share of the Capital Call by wire transfer of immediately available funds to an account designated by the Company within ninety (90) calendar days after receipt of the Capital Call Notice (the \"**Funding Deadline**\").

**(d) Failure to Fund; Dilution.**

> \(i\) If a Member fails to fund its pro-rata share of a Capital Call by the Funding Deadline (a \"**Capital Call Default**\"), the Company shall promptly deliver a written notice of default (the \"**Default Notice**\") to the Defaulting Member, specifying the amount of the unfunded Capital Call contribution and the consequences of failing to cure the Capital Call Default.
>
> \(ii\) The Defaulting Member shall have a cure period of ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the Default Notice (the \"**Cure Period**\") to fund the full amount of its unfunded pro-rata share of the Capital Call by wire transfer of immediately available funds to an account designated by the Company.
>
> \(iii\) If the Defaulting Member fails to cure the Capital Call Default within the Cure Period, the Non-Defaulting Member shall have the right (but not the obligation) to fund the entire unfunded amount of the Capital Call. Upon such funding by the Non-Defaulting Member, the Non-Defaulting Member may, at its election, treat the funded amount as either:
>
> \(A\) a loan to the Defaulting Member (the \"**Cure Loan**\"), bearing interest at the prime rate as published in *The Wall Street Journal* on the date of funding plus two percent (2%) per annum, with principal and accrued interest repayable on demand but in no event later than the earlier of (x) twelve (12) months from the date of the Cure Loan or (y) the next distribution to the Defaulting Member, which distribution shall be applied first to repayment of the Cure Loan; or
>
> \(B\) an additional Capital Contribution by the Non-Defaulting Member, in which case the Percentage Interests of the Members shall be adjusted as set forth in subsection (iv) below, subject to the dilution cap in subsection (v).
>
> \(iv\) If the Non-Defaulting Member elects to treat the funded amount as an additional Capital Contribution under subsection (iii)(B), the Percentage Interests of the Members shall be adjusted on a pro-rata basis to reflect the additional capital contributed by the Non-Defaulting Member relative to the aggregate Capital Contributions of both Members.
>
> \(v\) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Section 5.01(d), in no event shall the Defaulting Member\'s Percentage Interest be diluted by more than ten percent (10%) of such Member\'s then-current Percentage Interest in any single Fiscal Year (the \"**Annual Dilution Cap**\"). If the adjustment that would otherwise result from the application of subsection (iv) would exceed the Annual Dilution Cap, the excess Capital Contribution by the Non-Defaulting Member shall be treated as a Cure Loan under subsection (iii)(A).
>
> \(vi\) All governance rights (including the right to appoint Managers pursuant to Section 4.01), distribution entitlements, allocation rights, and all other rights and obligations attributable to Percentage Interests shall be automatically adjusted to reflect the revised Percentage Interests effective as of the date of funding by the Non-Defaulting Member. The Company shall promptly update its books and records to reflect the adjusted Percentage Interests and shall provide written notice thereof to each Member.

**[ARTICLE VI --- DISTRIBUTIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 6.01 --- Distributions Generally**

Distributions of Distributable Cash shall be made to Members in proportion to their respective Percentage Interests at such times and in such amounts as determined by the Management Committee in its sole discretion. The Management Committee shall consider the cash flow needs of the Company, including anticipated capital expenditures, working capital requirements, debt service obligations, and reserve requirements, in determining the amount and timing of distributions. Except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, no Member shall have the right to demand or receive any distribution from the Company.

**Section 6.02 --- Tax Distributions**

Notwithstanding Section 6.01, the Company shall make tax distributions (\"**Tax Distributions**\") to each Member no later than fifteen (15) days prior to each estimated tax payment date (i.e., April 15, June 15, September 15, and January 15 of each year, or such other dates as may be applicable) in an amount equal to the product of (i) the net taxable income of the Company allocated to such Member for the applicable period and (ii) the highest combined marginal federal and state income tax rate applicable to an individual resident in New York, New York, or such other tax rate as the Management Committee may determine from time to time to be appropriate. Tax Distributions made pursuant to this Section 6.02 shall be treated as advances on, and shall be credited against, any subsequent distributions to which such Member would otherwise be entitled under Section 6.01. In the event that a Tax Distribution to any Member exceeds the aggregate amount of distributions that such Member would otherwise be entitled to receive under this Agreement, such excess shall not be recoverable from such Member.

**Section 6.03 --- Restrictions on Distributions**

No distribution shall be made by the Company to any Member if, after giving effect to such distribution, (i) the Company would not be able to pay its debts as they become due in the ordinary course of business, or (ii) the Company\'s total assets would be less than the sum of its total liabilities, or (iii) such distribution would otherwise violate the Delaware LLC Act or any other applicable law. The Management Committee shall have the authority to withhold distributions to any Member to the extent necessary to comply with applicable tax withholding requirements.

**[ARTICLE VII --- ALLOCATIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 7.01 --- Profits and Losses**

Net Profits and Net Losses of the Company for each Fiscal Year (or other applicable period) shall be allocated among the Members in proportion to their respective Percentage Interests as of the last day of such Fiscal Year (or other applicable period), unless a different allocation is required by the special allocation provisions of Section 7.02 or the tax allocation provisions of Section 7.03.

**Section 7.02 --- Special Allocations**

Notwithstanding Section 7.01, the following special allocations shall be made in the following order of priority:

**(a) Minimum Gain Chargeback.** If there is a net decrease in the Company\'s minimum gain (as defined in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(b)(2)) during any Fiscal Year, each Member shall be allocated items of income and gain for such Fiscal Year in the manner and to the extent required by Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(f).

**(b) Member Nonrecourse Deductions.** Any Member nonrecourse deductions (as defined in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(i)(1)) for any Fiscal Year shall be allocated to the Member who bears the economic risk of loss with respect to the Member nonrecourse debt to which such Member nonrecourse deductions are attributable, in accordance with Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(i)(1).

**(c) Qualified Income Offset.** If any Member unexpectedly receives any adjustments, allocations, or distributions described in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d)(4), (5), or (6), items of Company income and gain shall be specially allocated to such Member in an amount and manner sufficient to eliminate, to the extent required by the Treasury Regulations, the adjusted capital account deficit (as determined in accordance with Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(ii)(d)) of such Member as quickly as possible.

**(d) Nonrecourse Deductions.** Nonrecourse deductions (as defined in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-2(b)(1)) for any Fiscal Year shall be allocated among the Members in proportion to their respective Percentage Interests.

**(e) Curative Allocations.** The allocations set forth in Sections 7.02(a) through 7.02(d) above (the \"**Regulatory Allocations**\") are intended to comply with certain requirements of Treasury Regulation §§ 1.704-1(b) and 1.704-2. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Article VII (other than the Regulatory Allocations), the Regulatory Allocations shall be taken into account in allocating Net Profits, Net Losses, and other items of income, gain, loss, and deduction among the Members so that, to the extent possible, the net amount of such allocations of other items and the Regulatory Allocations to each Member shall be equal to the net amount that would have been allocated to each Member if the Regulatory Allocations had not occurred.

**Section 7.03 --- Tax Allocations; Section 704(c)**

**(a)** Except as otherwise provided in this Section 7.03, for federal, state, and local income tax purposes, each item of income, gain, loss, deduction, and credit of the Company shall be allocated among the Members in the same manner as the corresponding item is allocated for Capital Account purposes under Sections 7.01 and 7.02.

**(b)** In accordance with Section 704(c) of the Internal Revenue Code and the applicable Treasury Regulations, income, gain, loss, and deduction with respect to property contributed to the Company by a Member shall, solely for federal, state, and local income tax purposes, be allocated among the Members so as to take into account any variation between the adjusted basis of such property to the Company for federal income tax purposes and the initial agreed value of such property as reflected in the contributing Member\'s Capital Account. The Members agree that the Company shall use the traditional method set forth in Treasury Regulation § 1.704-3(b) for the allocation of items of income, gain, loss, and deduction with respect to contributed property. Without limiting the foregoing, the Meridian Contributed IP and the Kastor supply-agreement commitments shall be allocated in accordance with this Section 7.03(b).

**(c)** If the agreed value of any Company asset is adjusted pursuant to Treasury Regulation § 1.704-1(b)(2)(iv)(f), subsequent allocations of income, gain, loss, and deduction with respect to such asset shall take account of any variation between the adjusted basis of such asset for federal income tax purposes and its adjusted agreed value in the same manner as under Section 704(c) of the Internal Revenue Code and the applicable Treasury Regulations.

**(d)** Allocations pursuant to this Section 7.03 are made solely for federal, state, and local income tax purposes and shall not affect, or in any way be taken into account in computing, any Member\'s Capital Account or share of Net Profits, Net Losses, other items, or distributions pursuant to any provision of this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE VIII --- BOOKS, RECORDS, AND ACCOUNTING]{.underline}**

**Section 8.01 --- Books and Records**

The Company shall maintain full and complete books and records of the Company\'s business, operations, and financial condition at the Company\'s principal office located at 4710 Industrial Commons Drive, Houston, Texas 77045, or at such other place as the Management Committee may from time to time designate. All books and records shall be maintained in accordance with GAAP, consistently applied. The books and records of the Company shall include, without limitation, the following: (i) a current list of the full name and last known business, residence, or mailing address of each Member; (ii) a copy of the Certificate of Formation and all amendments thereto; (iii) copies of this Agreement and all amendments thereto; (iv) copies of the Company\'s federal, state, and local income tax returns and reports for the three (3) most recent Fiscal Years; (v) copies of the Company\'s audited financial statements for the three (3) most recent Fiscal Years; (vi) copies of all minutes of meetings and written consents of the Management Committee and the Members; and (vii) such other records as may be required by the Delaware LLC Act.

**Section 8.02 --- Auditor**

Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP shall serve as the independent auditor of the Company. The independent auditor shall be engaged by the Company to audit the financial statements of the Company for each Fiscal Year and to deliver its audit report thereon. Audited financial statements, including a balance sheet, statement of income and expenses, statement of cash flows, and statement of Members\' equity, together with the independent auditor\'s report, shall be prepared and delivered to each Member within ninety (90) days after the end of each Fiscal Year. The Management Committee may change the independent auditor at any time, subject to the approval of the Management Committee as an ordinary matter under Section 4.03(a).

**Section 8.03 --- Inspection Rights**

Each Member and its duly authorized representatives (including accountants, attorneys, and financial advisors) shall have reasonable access to, and the right to inspect and copy, the books and records of the Company at any time and from time to time during normal business hours and upon reasonable prior written notice to the Company. Such inspection rights shall include the right to examine all financial records, contracts, correspondence, and other documents of the Company. The Company shall cooperate with, and make its officers, employees, and agents available to, the Members and their representatives in connection with any such inspection. The costs and expenses incurred by a Member in exercising its inspection rights under this Section 8.03 shall be borne by such Member.

**Section 8.04 --- Tax Returns**

The Tax Matters Member (initially Meridian) shall be responsible for the preparation, or shall cause to be prepared, all federal, state, and local tax returns and information statements of the Company for each Fiscal Year, and shall file such returns and statements in a timely manner. The Tax Matters Member shall deliver to each Member a copy of each Member\'s Schedule K-1 (or equivalent form) within seventy-five (75) days after the end of each Fiscal Year. The Tax Matters Member shall have the authority to make all elections and take all actions on behalf of the Company with respect to tax matters, including elections under Sections 754 and 761 of the Internal Revenue Code, provided that the Tax Matters Member shall consult with the other Member prior to making any election or taking any action that could reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on such other Member\'s tax position. All costs and expenses incurred in connection with the preparation, filing, and audit of the Company\'s tax returns shall be borne by the Company.

**[ARTICLE IX --- TRANSFERS OF MEMBERSHIP INTERESTS]{.underline}**

**Section 9.01 --- Restrictions on Transfer**

No Member shall Transfer all or any portion of its Membership Interest except in strict compliance with this Article IX. Any purported Transfer of a Membership Interest that is not made in compliance with this Article IX shall be null and void and of no force or effect, and the Company shall not recognize or give effect to any such purported Transfer. No Transfer of a Membership Interest shall be made if such Transfer would (i) violate any applicable federal or state securities laws, (ii) cause the Company to be treated as a publicly traded partnership within the meaning of Section 7704 of the Internal Revenue Code, (iii) cause the Company to be classified as a corporation for federal income tax purposes, or (iv) cause the termination of the Company under Section 708 of the Internal Revenue Code.

**Section 9.02 --- Permitted Transfers**

Notwithstanding Section 9.01, a Member may Transfer all or any portion of its Membership Interest to an Affiliate of such Member (a \"**Permitted Transfer**\"), provided that (i) the transferring Member delivers prior written notice to the other Member and the Company not less than fifteen (15) Business Days prior to the proposed Transfer, specifying the identity of the transferee Affiliate and the terms of the proposed Transfer, (ii) the transferee Affiliate executes a joinder agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the other Member, pursuant to which such transferee Affiliate agrees to be bound by all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement as a Member, (iii) the transferring Member shall remain jointly and severally liable for all obligations of the transferee Affiliate under this Agreement, and (iv) such Transfer complies with all applicable laws. If at any time a transferee Affiliate ceases to be an Affiliate of the transferring Member, such transferee shall be deemed to have offered its Membership Interest for sale pursuant to Section 9.03 as of the date on which it ceases to be an Affiliate.

**Section 9.03 --- Right of First Offer**

**(a)** If a Member (the \"**Offering Member**\") desires to Transfer all or any portion of its Membership Interest to any Person other than an Affiliate (a \"**Third-Party Transfer**\"), the Offering Member shall first deliver to the other Member (the \"**Non-Offering Member**\") a written notice (the \"**Offer Notice**\") setting forth (i) the Membership Interest proposed to be Transferred (including the Percentage Interest represented thereby), (ii) the proposed purchase price and all other material terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer, and (iii) the identity of the proposed third-party transferee (to the extent known).

**(b)** The Non-Offering Member shall have a period of forty-five (45) days following receipt of the Offer Notice (the \"**ROFO Period**\") within which to deliver to the Offering Member a written notice (the \"**Acceptance Notice**\") irrevocably electing to purchase the Membership Interest identified in the Offer Notice on the same terms and conditions set forth therein. If the Non-Offering Member delivers an Acceptance Notice within the ROFO Period, the closing of such purchase shall occur within sixty (60) days after delivery of the Acceptance Notice, or such later date as may be necessary to obtain required regulatory approvals.

**(c)** If the Non-Offering Member does not deliver an Acceptance Notice within the ROFO Period, or affirmatively declines to purchase the offered Membership Interest, the Offering Member shall be free, for a period of one hundred eighty (180) days following the expiration of the ROFO Period, to Transfer the offered Membership Interest to the proposed third-party transferee identified in the Offer Notice on terms no more favorable to such transferee than those set forth in the Offer Notice. If such Transfer is not consummated within such one hundred eighty (180) day period, the Offering Member\'s right to Transfer without compliance with this Section 9.03 shall expire, and any subsequent proposed Transfer shall again be subject to the provisions of this Section 9.03.

**Section 9.04 --- Tag-Along Rights**

**(a)** If Meridian proposes to Transfer all or a portion of its Membership Interest to a third party (a \"**Tag-Along Sale**\"), Meridian shall deliver to Kastor a written notice (the \"**Tag-Along Notice**\") setting forth the terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer, including the identity of the proposed purchaser, the purchase price, and all other material terms and conditions, not less than thirty (30) days prior to the proposed closing of such Transfer.

**(b)** Kastor shall have the right (but not the obligation), exercisable by written notice to Meridian within twenty (20) days after receipt of the Tag-Along Notice, to include all or a pro-rata portion of its Membership Interest in such Transfer on the same terms and conditions (including price per unit of Membership Interest) as those applicable to Meridian\'s Transfer. If Kastor elects to participate in the Tag-Along Sale, Meridian shall use commercially reasonable efforts to cause the proposed purchaser to acquire Kastor\'s Membership Interest on such terms. Meridian shall not consummate the proposed Transfer unless the proposed purchaser agrees to purchase Kastor\'s Membership Interest on such terms.

**(c)** If Kastor does not deliver a written notice of its election to participate in the Tag-Along Sale within the twenty (20) day period specified in Section 9.04(b), Kastor shall be deemed to have waived its tag-along rights with respect to such Transfer.

**Section 9.05 --- Drag-Along Rights**

**(a)** If Members holding at least fifty-five percent (55%) of the aggregate Membership Interests (the \"**Dragging Members**\") approve a sale or other Transfer of all (but not less than all) of the Membership Interests of the Company to a bona fide third-party purchaser that is not an Affiliate of any Member (a \"**Drag-Along Sale**\"), the Dragging Members may require all other Members (each, a \"**Dragged Member**\") to participate in such Drag-Along Sale and to sell their Membership Interests to such third-party purchaser on the same terms and conditions as those applicable to the Dragging Members.

**(b)** The Dragging Members shall deliver to each Dragged Member a written notice (the \"**Drag-Along Notice**\") not less than thirty (30) days prior to the proposed closing of the Drag-Along Sale, setting forth the identity of the proposed purchaser, the purchase price, and all other material terms and conditions of the proposed sale.
