Each Dragged Member shall be obligated to execute and deliver such agreements, instruments, and other documents as may be reasonably required to consummate the Drag-Along Sale, including customary representations, warranties, indemnities, and covenants, provided that (i) the liability of each Dragged Member for representations, warranties, and indemnities (other than those relating to such Dragged Member\'s title to and authority to transfer its Membership Interest) shall be limited to such Dragged Member\'s pro-rata share (based on relative Percentage Interests) of any aggregate liability, (ii) no Dragged Member shall be required to provide non-competition, non-solicitation, or similar restrictive covenant obligations in connection with the Drag-Along Sale, except to the extent already applicable under Article XIV, and (iii) the purchase price shall be allocated among the Members in proportion to their respective Percentage Interests.

**Section 9.06 --- Involuntary Transfers**

In the event of any involuntary Transfer of a Member\'s Membership Interest, whether by operation of law, foreclosure, bankruptcy, attachment, levy, garnishment, judicial order, or otherwise (an \"**Involuntary Transfer**\"), the transferee or the Bankrupt Member (as applicable) shall give written notice thereof to the Company and the other Member promptly upon such Involuntary Transfer. The Membership Interest subject to such Involuntary Transfer shall be subject to the buyout provisions of Article X, and the non-transferring Member shall have the right to purchase such Membership Interest in accordance with the procedures and valuation methodology set forth in Article X. The transferee in any Involuntary Transfer shall take the Membership Interest subject to all of the terms and conditions of this Agreement and shall not be admitted as a Member unless and until such transferee executes a joinder agreement in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the remaining Member.

**[ARTICLE X --- EXIT; BUYOUT PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 10.01 --- Triggering Events**

A buyout or dissolution procedure under this Article X shall be initiated upon the occurrence of one of the following events (each, a \"**Triggering Event**\"):

**(a)** A Change of Control of either Member;

**(b)** A Material Breach by either Member that remains uncured after the applicable cure period set forth in Section 11.02 ;

**(c)** A Deadlock that has not been resolved through the escalation and mediation procedures set forth in Section 4.04(a) and (b); or

**(d)** An Involuntary Transfer of all or any portion of a Member\'s Membership Interest (by operation of law, bankruptcy, judicial order, or otherwise).

For the avoidance of doubt, the occurrence of an Involuntary Transfer shall be subject to both the provisions of Section 9.06 and this Article X.

**Section 10.02 --- Buyout Procedure**

**(a)** Upon the occurrence of a Triggering Event, the non-triggering Member (the \"**Initiating Member**\") shall have the right, but not the obligation, exercisable by written notice to the other Member (the \"**Selling Member**\") within sixty (60) days after the occurrence of the Triggering Event (or, in the case of a Material Breach, within sixty (60) days after the expiration of the applicable cure period without cure), to elect to purchase the Membership Interest of the Selling Member at the Fair Market Value of such Membership Interest as determined in accordance with Section 10.03.

**(b)** The closing of any buyout under this Section 10.02 shall occur within ninety (90) days after the determination of Fair Market Value pursuant to Section 10.03, subject to the receipt of all necessary regulatory and governmental approvals and the satisfaction of all closing conditions reasonably required by the Initiating Member. At the closing, the Selling Member shall deliver to the Initiating Member (or its designee) such instruments of assignment and transfer as may be necessary to convey the Selling Member\'s Membership Interest to the Initiating Member (or its designee), free and clear of all liens and encumbrances (other than those imposed by this Agreement and applicable securities laws), and the Initiating Member shall deliver to the Selling Member the purchase price in accordance with Section 10.04.

**Section 10.03 --- Valuation**

**(a) Methodology.** The Fair Market Value of the Company shall be determined by the Independent Appraiser (as defined below) using a discounted cash flow (\"**DCF**\") analysis as the primary valuation methodology, supplemented by such other methodologies (including comparable transaction analysis and EBITDA multiples) as the Independent Appraiser deems appropriate in the exercise of its professional judgment. The Independent Appraiser shall deliver a written valuation report setting forth its determination and the bases therefor.

**(b) Selection of Independent Appraiser.**

\(i\) The Members shall endeavor to agree upon a single nationally recognized independent accounting or valuation firm (a \"Big Four Firm\" or equivalent) to serve as the Independent Appraiser within thirty (30) calendar days following the occurrence of the applicable Triggering Event.

\(ii\) If the Members are unable to agree on a single Independent Appraiser within such thirty (30)-day period, each Member shall, within fifteen (15) calendar days thereafter, select one nationally recognized independent accounting or valuation firm, and the two firms so selected shall, within fifteen (15) calendar days of their appointment, jointly select a third nationally recognized independent accounting or valuation firm to serve as the Independent Appraiser.

\(iii\) If the two firms cannot agree on a third firm within the fifteen (15)-day period specified above, either Member may apply to the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware for the appointment of the Independent Appraiser.

\(iv\) The Independent Appraiser shall not be the Company\'s then-current auditor (currently Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP) or any firm that has provided material services to either Member during the preceding twenty-four (24) months.

**(c) Baseball Arbitration.** If either Member disputes the Independent Appraiser\'s determination of Fair Market Value, the following procedure shall apply:

\(i\) Each Member shall, within twenty (20) calendar days of receipt of the Independent Appraiser\'s report, submit to the Independent Appraiser a single sealed final valuation number (each, a \"**Final Offer**\").

\(ii\) The Independent Appraiser shall select the Final Offer that is closest to its own determination of Fair Market Value, and the selected Final Offer shall be the Fair Market Value for all purposes under this Agreement.

\(iii\) The Independent Appraiser\'s selection shall be final and binding upon the Members, absent fraud or manifest error.

**(d) Member Interest Valuation.** The Fair Market Value of a Member\'s Membership Interest shall equal the Fair Market Value of the Company multiplied by such Member\'s Percentage Interest. No minority discount or marketability discount shall be applied.

**(e) Costs.** The costs and expenses of the Independent Appraiser shall be borne equally by the Members.

**Section 10.04 --- Payment Terms**

The purchase price determined pursuant to Section 10.03 shall be payable by the Initiating Member to the Selling Member at the closing of the buyout as follows: (i) at least seventy percent (70%) of the purchase price shall be paid in cash by wire transfer of immediately available funds to an account designated by the Selling Member; and (ii) at the Initiating Member\'s election, up to thirty percent (30%) of the purchase price may be paid in the form of a promissory note issued by the Initiating Member (or its designee) in favor of the Selling Member, bearing interest at the prime rate as published by *The Wall Street Journal* on the date of closing, plus two percent (2.00%) per annum, payable in equal quarterly installments of principal and accrued interest over a period of twenty-four (24) months following the closing date, with the first installment due ninety (90) days after the closing date. Such promissory note shall be unsecured unless otherwise agreed by the parties and shall contain customary terms and conditions, including provisions for acceleration upon default.

**[ARTICLE XI --- EVENTS OF DEFAULT; MATERIAL BREACH]{.underline}**

**Section 11.01 --- Material Breach**

A \"**Material Breach**\" shall mean the occurrence of any one or more of the following events:

**(a)** A material breach of any representation, warranty, or covenant of a Member under this Agreement, including any failure by a Member to comply with its obligations under Article III (Capital Contributions), Article IX (Transfers), Article XIV (Non-Competition; Non-Solicitation), or Article XV (Representations and Warranties);

**(b)** A material breach of the exclusivity or non-compete obligations of a Member under Article XIV, including any investment in, employment by, or provision of services to a Restricted Competitor or any other Person engaged in the Restricted Business within the Restricted Territory;

**(c)** The commission of fraud, willful misconduct, or gross negligence by a Member or any Manager appointed by such Member in connection with the Company\'s business, operations, or affairs; or

**(d)** A Capital Call Default that results in dilution exceeding ten percent (10%) of the Defaulting Member\'s original Percentage Interest (as determined as of the Effective Date), whether as a result of a single Capital Call Default or a series of Capital Call Defaults.

**Section 11.02 --- Notice and Cure**

**(a)** Upon the occurrence of an event that the non-breaching Member believes constitutes a Material Breach, the non-breaching Member shall deliver written notice (the \"**Breach Notice**\") to the breaching Member specifying the nature of the Material Breach in reasonable detail, including the specific provision(s) of this Agreement that have been breached and the facts and circumstances giving rise to such breach.

**(b)** The breaching Member shall have a period of thirty (30) calendar days following receipt of the Breach Notice to cure such Material Breach; provided, however, that if such Material Breach is of a nature that is not reasonably capable of cure within thirty (30) calendar days, the breaching Member shall have a period not to exceed sixty (60) calendar days following receipt of the Breach Notice to effect such cure, provided that the breaching Member has commenced to cure such Material Breach within the initial thirty (30) day period and is diligently prosecuting such cure to completion.

**(c)** If the breaching Member cures the Material Breach within the applicable cure period, the Breach Notice shall be deemed withdrawn and the Material Breach shall be deemed to have been cured for all purposes of this Agreement.

**Section 11.03 --- Remedies**

Upon the failure of the breaching Member to cure a Material Breach within the applicable cure period set forth in Section 11.02, the non-breaching Member shall be entitled to exercise any one or more of the following remedies:

**(a)** Exercise of the buyout right under Article X, including the right to purchase the Membership Interest of the breaching Member at Fair Market Value in accordance with the procedures set forth in Section 10.02;

**(b)** Pursuit of damages at law or in equity, including specific performance, injunctive relief, or any other remedy available under applicable law or this Agreement; and

**(c)** Exercise of any other right or remedy available to the non-breaching Member under this Agreement, the Delaware LLC Act, or applicable law.

The rights and remedies set forth in this Section 11.03 are cumulative and not exclusive and shall be in addition to any other rights and remedies available to the non-breaching Member at law, in equity, or otherwise.

**[ARTICLE XII --- INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**Section 12.01 --- Contributed IP**

**(a) Meridian Contributed IP.** Meridian contributes to the Company the twenty-three (23) items of Intellectual Property identified in Schedule 12.01, Part A (the \"**Meridian Contributed IP**\"), including proprietary coating formulations embedded in JV products. The Meridian Contributed IP has been independently appraised by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP at an aggregate value of Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000). A complete description of each item of Meridian Contributed IP, including any applicable registration or patent application numbers and individual assigned values, is set forth in Schedule 12.01, Part A.

**(b) Kastor Contributed IP.** Kastor contributes to the Company the nine (9) items of Intellectual Property identified in Schedule 12.01, Part B (the \"**Kastor Contributed IP**\"), including manufacturing process documentation used in JV operations. A complete description of each item of Kastor Contributed IP, including any applicable registration or patent application numbers, is set forth in Schedule 12.01, Part B.

**(c) License to Company.** Each Member hereby grants to the Company an exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license to use, practice, and exploit such Member\'s Contributed IP solely for the Business Purpose during the term of this Agreement. The foregoing license shall be non-transferable and non-sublicensable (except to subcontractors of the Company engaged in furtherance of the Business Purpose, and only to the extent necessary for the performance of their services to the Company). The license granted under this Section 12.01(c) shall terminate automatically upon the dissolution of the Company, termination of this Agreement, or an Exit Event, subject to the reversion provisions of Section 12.03.

**Section 12.02 --- Co-Developed IP; Ownership**

All Co-Developed IP (as described in Schedule 12.02) developed by or on behalf of the Company after the Effective Date using Contributed IP, Company resources, or both, shall be owned exclusively by the Company. Neither Member shall have any ownership interest in Co-Developed IP independent of its Membership Interest in the Company. Each Member agrees to assign, and hereby assigns, to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to any Co-Developed IP that may be deemed to vest initially in such Member or any of its employees, agents, or contractors. Each Member shall cause its employees, agents, and contractors who participate in the development of Co-Developed IP to execute appropriate invention assignment agreements in favor of the Company. The procedures for recording, reporting, and classifying Co-Developed IP are set forth in Schedule 12.02.

**Section 12.03 --- Reversion of Contributed IP**

Upon the dissolution of the Company, termination of this Agreement, or an Exit Event, all Contributed IP shall automatically revert to the contributing Member, free and clear of any lien, encumbrance, security interest, or other restriction created by or through the Company. Such reversion shall be effective immediately upon the applicable event without the requirement of any further action, instrument, agreement, or consent by the Company, any Member, or any other Person. For the avoidance of doubt, upon such reversion, the Company\'s license to the Contributed IP under Section 12.01(c) shall terminate, and no transitional license to the Company or the other Member shall be granted or implied with respect to the reverting Contributed IP.

**Section 12.04 --- Co-Developed IP License-Back**

**(a)** Upon the dissolution of the Company, termination of this Agreement, or an Exit Event, the Company (or its successor or assign) hereby grants to each Member a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable license to use, make, have made, sell, offer to sell, import, and otherwise exploit all Co-Developed IP (the \"**License-Back**\").

**(b) Territorial Scope.** Each Member\'s License-Back shall extend to such Member\'s respective territory:

\(i\) **Meridian\'s Territory:** the United States, Canada, and Mexico (collectively, \"**North America**\").

\(ii\) **Kastor\'s Territory:** all countries and territories outside of North America (\"**Rest of World**\").

**(c) Sublicensing.** Each Member shall have the right to sublicense the Co-Developed IP within its respective territory to any third party without restriction and without the prior consent of the other Member, provided that such sublicense shall be consistent with the territorial limitations set forth in subsection (b).

**(d) No Cross-Territorial Rights.** Neither Member shall exercise or sublicense any rights under the License-Back in the other Member\'s territory without the prior written consent of such other Member.

**Section 12.05 --- Restricted Competitors**

Schedule 12.05 shall set forth an initial list of Restricted Competitors, which are Persons engaged in the specialty polymer coatings industry that are designated as competitors of the Company for purposes of this Article XII and Article XIV. The Management Committee may update, supplement, or modify the list of Restricted Competitors from time to time by a majority vote of the Management Committee. Any updates to the list of Restricted Competitors shall be promptly reflected in an amended Schedule 12.05 and delivered to each Member.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- DISSOLUTION AND WINDING UP]{.underline}**

**Section 13.01 --- Events of Dissolution**

The Company shall be dissolved and its affairs wound up only upon the occurrence of one of the following events:

**(a)** The written consent of all Members to dissolve the Company;

**(b)** The entry of a judicial decree of dissolution of the Company under Section 18-802 of the Delaware LLC Act or any successor provision; or

**(c)** The completion of a buyout under Article X that results in a single remaining Member who elects not to continue the business of the Company. For the avoidance of doubt, if a buyout under Article X results in a single remaining Member, such Member may elect in its sole discretion to continue the business of the Company as a single-member limited liability company, in which case the Company shall not be dissolved.

No other event, including without limitation the death, retirement, resignation, expulsion, bankruptcy, insolvency, or dissolution of any Member, shall cause the dissolution of the Company unless such event gives rise to one of the foregoing events of dissolution.

**Section 13.02 --- Winding Up**

Upon dissolution, the Management Committee (or, if no Managers remain, a liquidating trustee appointed by the Members or by a court of competent jurisdiction) shall wind up the affairs of the Company and distribute the assets of the Company in the following order of priority:

> \(i\) First, to the payment and discharge of all debts and liabilities of the Company owed to creditors, including debts and liabilities owed to Members in their capacities as creditors (but not in their capacities as Members), in the order of priority established by applicable law;
>
> \(ii\) Second, to the establishment of such reserves as the Management Committee (or liquidating trustee) may determine to be reasonably necessary for contingent, conditional, or unmatured liabilities and obligations of the Company, which reserves shall be held by the Company for such period as the Management Committee (or liquidating trustee) shall determine to be appropriate and shall thereafter be distributed as provided in clauses (iii) and (iv) below;
>
> \(iii\) Third, to the return to each Member of the balance of such Member\'s Capital Account (after giving effect to all allocations of Net Profits, Net Losses, and other items for the Fiscal Year in which the dissolution occurs); and
>
> \(iv\) Fourth, to the distribution of any remaining assets to the Members in proportion to their respective Percentage Interests.

All distributions in kind shall be made at the fair market value of the assets distributed (net of any liabilities secured by or otherwise attributable to such assets), as determined in good faith by the Management Committee (or liquidating trustee). Any gain or loss realized upon the disposition of Company assets in connection with the winding up shall be allocated among the Members in accordance with Article VII.

**Section 13.03 --- Reversion of Contributed IP**

Upon the dissolution of the Company, all Contributed IP shall revert to the contributing Member in accordance with the provisions of Section 12.03 of this Agreement. Such reversion shall be effective immediately upon dissolution and shall not be subject to the distribution waterfall set forth in Section 13.02.

**[ARTICLE XIV --- NON-COMPETITION; NON-SOLICITATION]{.underline}**

**Section 14.01 --- Non-Competition**

**(a) During the Term.** During the term of this Agreement, neither Member nor any of its Affiliates shall, directly or indirectly, engage in, participate in, assist, invest in (other than passive investments of less than five percent (5%) of the publicly traded equity securities of any Person), lend money to, guarantee the obligations of, or provide management, consulting, technical, or other services to any Person engaged in the Restricted Business within the Restricted Territory. This restriction shall apply to all activities conducted by a Member or its Affiliates, whether as an owner, operator, partner, joint venturer, shareholder, member, investor, lender, licensor, licensee, consultant, advisor, agent, employee, or in any other capacity.

**(b) Post-Exit.** For a period of twelve (12) months following the effective date of a Member\'s exit from the Company by any means (whether through buyout, dissolution, Transfer, or otherwise) (the \"**Restricted Period**\"), the exiting Member and its Affiliates shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in, participate in, assist, invest in (other than passive investments of less than five percent (5%) of the publicly traded equity securities of any Person), lend money to, guarantee the obligations of, or provide management, consulting, technical, or other services to any Person engaged in the Restricted Business within the Restricted Territory.

**(c) Restricted Business.** For purposes of this Section 14.01, \"**Restricted Business**\" means the research, development, manufacture, marketing, distribution, or sale of the specific product lines actively developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company as of the date of the applicable Member\'s exit, as identified by reference to the Company\'s then-current product catalog and revenue records.

**(d) Restricted Territory.** For purposes of this Section 14.01, \"**Restricted Territory**\" means the geographic territories in which the Company had active sales (defined as recorded revenues exceeding One Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$100,000) in the trailing twelve (12) months) as of the date of the applicable Member\'s exit.

**Section 14.02 --- Non-Solicitation**

For a period of twelve (12) months following the effective date of a Member\'s exit from the Company by any means (whether through buyout, dissolution, Transfer, or otherwise), neither the exiting Member nor any of its Affiliates shall, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment, recruit, hire, or otherwise engage (whether as an employee, independent contractor, consultant, or otherwise) any individual who was an employee of the Company at any time during the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the effective date of such Member\'s exit, without the prior written consent of the remaining Member or the Company. The foregoing restriction shall not apply to (i) general solicitations of employment not specifically targeted at employees of the Company, including advertisements in newspapers, trade publications, or online job boards, provided that the exiting Member or its Affiliates do not hire any employee of the Company who responds to such general solicitation without the prior written consent of the remaining Member or the Company, or (ii) any employee of the Company who was terminated by the Company prior to the date of solicitation by the exiting Member or its Affiliates.

**Section 14.03 --- Reasonableness; Severability**

The Members acknowledge and agree that (i) the duration, scope, and geographic limitations of the restrictions set forth in this Article XIV are reasonable and necessary to protect the Company\'s legitimate business interests, including the protection of Contributed IP, Co-Developed IP, trade secrets, confidential information, customer relationships, and goodwill, (ii) the Members have received adequate consideration for such restrictions in the form of their participation in the Company and the benefits derived therefrom, and (iii) the restrictions set forth in this Article XIV are an essential element of this Agreement and the willingness of each Member to enter into this Agreement and make its Capital Contributions. If any court of competent jurisdiction determines that any restriction set forth in this Article XIV is unenforceable as to duration, scope, geographic area, or otherwise, such restriction shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to render it enforceable, and as so modified shall be enforced to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law. Each of the restrictions set forth in this Article XIV shall be construed as severable and independent of every other restriction, and the unenforceability of any one restriction shall not affect the enforceability of any other restriction.

**[ARTICLE XV --- REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES]{.underline}**

**Section 15.01 --- Mutual Representations**

Each Member hereby represents and warrants to the other Member and to the Company, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a) Due Organization and Valid Existence.** Such Member is a limited liability company (in the case of Meridian) or a corporation (in the case of Kastor) duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the laws of the jurisdiction of its formation or incorporation, and has all requisite power and authority to own, lease, and operate its properties and to carry on its business as presently conducted and as proposed to be conducted.

**(b) Authority.** Such Member has full power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform this Agreement and all other agreements, instruments, and documents to be executed and delivered by it in connection herewith. The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Member have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of such Member, including the approval of its board of directors (or equivalent governing body) and any required member, shareholder, or partner approvals.

**(c) No Conflict.** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Member does not and will not (i) conflict with or violate any provision of such Member\'s certificate of formation, certificate of incorporation, operating agreement, bylaws, articles of association, or other organizational documents, (ii) conflict with or violate any applicable law, rule, regulation, judgment, order, decree, or governmental permit or license applicable to such Member, or (iii) conflict with, result in a breach of, constitute a default under, or give rise to any right of termination, cancellation, or acceleration under, any material contract, agreement, instrument, or obligation to which such Member is a party or by which its properties or assets are bound.

**(d) No Litigation.** There is no action, suit, proceeding, claim, arbitration, investigation, or inquiry pending or, to such Member\'s knowledge, threatened against or affecting such Member before any court, arbitrator, or governmental or regulatory authority that would materially impair or adversely affect such Member\'s ability to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

**Section 15.02 --- Meridian\'s Representations**

Meridian hereby represents and warrants to Kastor and to the Company, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a) Ownership of Contributed IP.** Meridian is the sole and exclusive owner of, or has the right to contribute, all Meridian Contributed IP identified in Schedule 12.01, Part A, free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, security interests, options, claims, charges, and restrictions of any kind, and has the full right, power, and authority to contribute such Intellectual Property to the Company and to grant the license contemplated by Section 12.01(c).

**(b) Non-Infringement.** To Meridian\'s knowledge, the Meridian Contributed IP does not infringe, misappropriate, or otherwise violate any Intellectual Property rights of any third party. To Meridian\'s knowledge, no third party is infringing, misappropriating, or otherwise violating any Meridian Contributed IP.

**(c) Arm\'s-Length Appraisal.** The appraisal of the Meridian Contributed IP by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP at an aggregate value of Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000) was conducted at arm\'s length, in accordance with the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice, and without any undue influence or direction by Meridian. Meridian has no reason to believe that the appraised value of the Meridian Contributed IP is materially inaccurate.

**Section 15.03 --- Kastor\'s Representations**

Kastor hereby represents and warrants to Meridian and to the Company, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a) Authorization of Cash Contribution.** The cash Capital Contribution of Kastor in the amount of Forty-One Million Eight Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$41,800,000) has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of Kastor, including the approval of Kastor\'s board of directors and any required shareholder approvals, and Kastor has sufficient available funds to make such contribution at the Closing.

**(b) Supply-Agreement Commitments.** The supply-agreement commitments contributed by Kastor as described in Schedule 3.02(b) can be fulfilled by Kastor in accordance with their terms without violation of any applicable laws, rules, or regulations or any existing agreements, contracts, or obligations of Kastor or any of its Affiliates.

**(c) Sanctions Compliance.** Neither Kastor nor any of its Affiliates, directors, officers, employees, or agents is a Person that is, or is owned or controlled by Persons that are, (i) the subject of any sanctions administered or enforced by the U.S. Department of the Treasury\'s Office of Foreign Assets Control, the U.S. Department of State, the United Nations Security Council, the European Union, or the Swiss State Secretariat for Economic Affairs, or (ii) located, organized, or resident in a country or territory that is the subject of comprehensive sanctions. Kastor is not subject to any sanctions, export controls, or other legal restrictions that would restrict, prohibit, or impair its ability to transact with U.S. entities, make its Capital Contributions to the Company, or perform its obligations under this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE XVI --- GENERAL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 16.01 --- Governing Law**

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to any choice-of-law or conflict-of-law rules or provisions (whether of the State of Delaware or any other jurisdiction) that would cause the application of the laws of any jurisdiction other than the State of Delaware. The Members agree that the Delaware LLC Act shall apply to the internal affairs of the Company to the fullest extent permitted by law.

**Section 16.02 --- Dispute Resolution**

Any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement, including any dispute regarding the formation, existence, validity, breach, interpretation, performance, or termination of this Agreement, shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery declines to accept jurisdiction or lacks subject-matter jurisdiction over the matter in dispute, the Superior Court of the State of Delaware sitting in and for New Castle County), and each party hereby irrevocably and unconditionally consents to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such courts for the purpose of resolving any such dispute. Each party hereby irrevocably waives, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, (i) any objection that it may now or hereafter have to the laying of venue of any such action or proceeding in such courts, and (ii) any claim that any such action or proceeding brought in such courts has been brought in an inconvenient forum. Process in any such action or proceeding may be served on any party anywhere in the world, whether within or outside the jurisdiction of any such court. Without limiting the foregoing, each party agrees that service of process on such party as provided in Section 16.03 shall be deemed effective service of process on such party.

**Section 16.03 --- Notices**

All notices, requests, demands, consents, approvals, and other communications required or permitted to be given under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given or made if (i) delivered in person, (ii) sent by nationally recognized overnight courier (with tracking capability), or (iii) sent by email (with confirmation of receipt), in each case to the party at the address or email address set forth below, or at such other address or email address as such party may designate by written notice to the other party in accordance with this Section 16.03:

**Section 16.04 --- Entire Agreement**

This Agreement (including all Schedules and Exhibits attached hereto and incorporated herein by reference) constitutes the entire agreement of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior agreements, understandings, negotiations, discussions, representations, warranties, and commitments, whether oral or written, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. There are no representations, warranties, covenants, agreements, or conditions, express or implied, collateral or otherwise, relating to the subject matter hereof except as specifically set forth in this Agreement.

**Section 16.05 --- Amendments**

This Agreement may be amended, modified, or supplemented only by a written instrument executed and delivered by all Members. No amendment, modification, or supplement shall be effective unless it is in writing and signed by each of Meridian and Kastor. Any amendment, modification, or supplement effected in accordance with this Section 16.05 shall be binding upon each Member and the Company.

**Section 16.06 --- Waiver**

No waiver of any provision of this Agreement, and no consent to any departure therefrom, shall be effective unless in writing and signed by the waiving party. No waiver shall be deemed to be, or shall constitute, a waiver of any other provision of this Agreement, whether or not similar, nor shall any waiver constitute a continuing waiver. No failure or delay on the part of any party in exercising any right, power, or privilege under this Agreement shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise of any right, power, or privilege preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power, or privilege.

**Section 16.07 --- Severability**

If any provision of this Agreement (or any portion thereof) is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement (or the remaining portion thereof), all of which shall remain in full force and effect. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to replace any provision held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable with a valid, legal, and enforceable provision that, to the greatest extent possible, achieves the original intent and economic effect of the invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision.

**Section 16.08 --- Counterparts**

This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument. Delivery of an executed counterpart of this Agreement by facsimile transmission or by email in portable document format (PDF) shall be as effective as delivery of an originally executed counterpart.

**Section 16.09 --- Confidentiality**

**(a)** Each Member shall maintain the confidentiality of all non-public information relating to the Company, the other Member, and the business and affairs of the Company and the other Member (collectively, \"**Confidential Information**\"), and shall not disclose any Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the other Member, except as provided in this Section 16.09.

**(b)** The obligations of confidentiality set forth in this Section 16.09 shall not apply to Confidential Information that (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the receiving Member or its representatives in violation of this Agreement, (ii) was available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis prior to its disclosure by the disclosing Member, (iii) becomes available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing Member, provided that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement with the disclosing Member, or (iv) is independently developed by the receiving Member without reference to or use of the Confidential Information.

**(c)** Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Member may disclose Confidential Information (i) to its Affiliates, directors, officers, employees, accountants, attorneys, financial advisors, and other representatives who have a need to know such information in connection with the performance of such Member\'s obligations under this Agreement, provided that such Persons are bound by confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those set forth herein, (ii) to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, provided that the disclosing Member provides prompt written notice to the other Member and cooperates with the other Member\'s efforts to obtain a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or (iii) to the extent necessary in connection with the enforcement of such Member\'s rights under this Agreement.

**(d)** The obligations of confidentiality set forth in this Section 16.09 shall survive the termination of this Agreement and the dissolution of the Company for a period of five (5) years.

**Section 16.10 --- Further Assurances**

Each Member shall, from time to time after the Effective Date, execute and deliver such additional instruments, documents, conveyances, or assurances and take such further actions as may be reasonably necessary or desirable to carry out the provisions of this Agreement and to effectuate the transactions contemplated hereby, including the filing of any reports, returns, or other documents with any governmental authority required in connection with the formation, organization, and operation of the Company.

**Section 16.11 --- No Third-Party Beneficiaries**

This Agreement is for the sole benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors and permitted assigns, and nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, is intended to or shall confer upon any other Person any legal or equitable right, benefit, remedy, or claim of any kind under or by reason of this Agreement.

**SCHEDULE 2.04**

**BUSINESS PURPOSE**

The Business Purpose of MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\") shall be to engage in the following activities:

1\. **Research and Development.** The research, development, testing, optimization, and prototyping of advanced polymer coating formulations, application technologies, curing systems, surface preparation methods, and related technologies, including both solvent-based and water-based coating systems, powder coatings, UV-curable coatings, and hybrid polymer coating systems.

2\. **Manufacturing.** The manufacture, production, compounding, blending, packaging, labeling, and quality assurance of advanced polymer coating products, including the establishment and operation of manufacturing facilities, pilot plants, and testing laboratories.

3\. **Commercialization.** The marketing, distribution, sale, licensing, and servicing of advanced polymer coating products in domestic and international markets, including the development and management of distribution channels, customer relationships, and brand identity.

4\. **Industry Verticals.** Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Company shall target the following industry verticals and application areas:

> \(a\) **Industrial Coatings.** Protective and functional coatings for industrial equipment, pipelines, storage tanks, structural steel, and other industrial infrastructure;
>
> \(b\) **Aerospace Coatings.** High-performance coatings for aircraft structures, engines, interiors, and components, including coatings meeting applicable FAA, EASA, and military specifications;
>
> \(c\) **Automotive Coatings.** Coatings for automotive OEM and aftermarket applications, including primers, basecoats, clearcoats, and specialty functional coatings;
>
> \(d\) **Marine Coatings.** Anti-fouling, anti-corrosion, and protective coatings for marine vessels, offshore structures, and port infrastructure;
>
> \(e\) **Infrastructure Coatings.** Coatings for bridges, highways, water and wastewater treatment facilities, power generation facilities, and other public and private infrastructure.

5\. **Ancillary Activities.** The performance of any and all activities ancillary, incidental, or related to the foregoing, including without limitation the procurement and management of raw materials, equipment, and supplies; the acquisition, protection, and enforcement of Intellectual Property rights; the hiring and management of employees and contractors; the entry into contracts and agreements with customers, suppliers, distributors, and other business partners; the raising of capital and incurrence of indebtedness as permitted by this Agreement; and the performance of such other lawful activities as may be approved by the Management Committee from time to time.

**SCHEDULE 3.02(b)**

**KASTOR SUPPLY-AGREEMENT COMMITMENTS**

The following sets forth the supply-agreement commitments contributed by Kastor Chemical Industries AG (\"Kastor\") to MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\"), which commitments have been valued by the Members at Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000) in the aggregate:

**1. Description of Supply-Agreement Commitments.**

Kastor commits to supply to the Company certain raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates required for the manufacture of the Company\'s advanced polymer coating products, on the terms and conditions set forth below. These commitments represent binding obligations of Kastor to provide the Company with preferential access to Kastor\'s existing supply chain, manufacturing capacity, and procurement infrastructure.

**2. Raw Materials Covered.**

The supply-agreement commitments cover the following categories of raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates:

> \(a\) Specialty epoxy resins (including bisphenol-A and bisphenol-F based resins);
>
> \(b\) Polyurethane prepolymers and isocyanate hardeners;
>
> \(c\) Fluoropolymer dispersions and PTFE micropowders;
>
> \(d\) Specialty silicone-based additives and flow control agents;
>
> \(e\) High-purity pigments and functional fillers (including titanium dioxide, zinc phosphate, and micaceous iron oxide); and
>
> \(f\) Such other raw materials as the Members may agree in writing from time to time.

**3. Volume Commitments.**

Kastor commits to supply the Company with the quantities of raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates necessary to support the Company\'s manufacturing operations during the first three (3) years following the Company\'s operational launch, up to the maximum annual volumes set forth in the detailed volume schedules to be agreed by the parties and attached hereto as an addendum prior to the Closing.

**4. Pricing.**

All raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates supplied by Kastor to the Company shall be priced at Kastor\'s internal transfer cost plus a markup not to exceed five percent (5%), which pricing shall be reviewed and, if appropriate, adjusted annually by mutual agreement of the Members. Such pricing is intended to provide the Company with cost advantages relative to prevailing market pricing for comparable raw materials.

**5. Delivery Obligations.**

Kastor shall deliver all raw materials and specialty chemical intermediates to the Company\'s manufacturing facilities (initially located at 4710 Industrial Commons Drive, Houston, Texas 77045) in accordance with delivery schedules to be mutually agreed upon by the parties. Kastor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain adequate inventory levels and to provide the Company with priority fulfillment in the event of supply constraints.

**6. Valuation.**

The aggregate value of the supply-agreement commitments described herein has been determined by the Members to be Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000), based on the estimated cost savings and preferential access provided to the Company over the initial three-year operating period relative to arm\'s-length market pricing for comparable raw materials and services.

**SCHEDULE 12.02**

**CO-DEVELOPED IP**

**1. Definition and Scope.**

\"Co-Developed IP\" means all Intellectual Property developed by or on behalf of MKPoly Coatings, LLC (the \"Company\") after the Effective Date (August 22, 2025) using Contributed IP (whether Meridian Contributed IP or Kastor Contributed IP), Company resources (including Company personnel, facilities, equipment, funding, and other assets), or any combination thereof. Co-Developed IP includes, without limitation, new formulations, process improvements, product designs, software, testing methodologies, technical data, know-how, and all other Intellectual Property conceived, created, developed, reduced to practice, or otherwise generated in connection with the Company\'s operations and the Business Purpose.

**2. Ownership.**

All Co-Developed IP shall be owned exclusively by the Company. Neither Member shall have any ownership interest in Co-Developed IP independent of its Membership Interest in the Company.

**3. Identification and Cataloguing.**

The Chief Technology Officer of the Company (or such other officer as the Management Committee may designate) shall be responsible for establishing and maintaining a comprehensive registry of all Co-Developed IP (the \"**Co-Developed IP Registry**\"). The Co-Developed IP Registry shall include, at a minimum, the following information for each item of Co-Developed IP:

> \(a\) A unique identification number assigned to each item;
>
> \(b\) A description of the item, including its nature (e.g., formulation, process, software, data, know-how) and the technical field to which it relates;
>
> \(c\) The date on which the item was conceived, created, or first reduced to practice;
>
> \(d\) The identity of all individuals (whether employees, contractors, or agents of the Company or any Member) who contributed to the conception, creation, or development of the item;
>
> \(e\) An identification of any Contributed IP (whether Meridian Contributed IP or Kastor Contributed IP) that was used in the development of the item;
>
> \(f\) The status of any patent applications, trademark registrations, copyright registrations, or other filings associated with the item; and
>
> \(g\) Any other information that the Management Committee may require from time to time.

**4. Reporting.**

The Chief Technology Officer shall provide the Management Committee with a quarterly report summarizing all new Co-Developed IP added to the Co-Developed IP Registry during the preceding Fiscal Quarter, together with any updates to existing entries. Such reports shall be presented at the regularly scheduled quarterly meetings of the Management Committee.

**5. Protection and Enforcement.**

The Management Committee shall determine, in its discretion, the appropriate measures to protect and enforce the Company\'s rights in Co-Developed IP, including the filing of patent applications, trademark registrations, copyright registrations, and the implementation of trade secret protection measures. All costs and expenses incurred in connection with the protection and enforcement of Co-Developed IP shall be borne by the Company.

**6. Invention Assignment.**

Each Member shall cause its employees, agents, and contractors who participate in the development of Co-Developed IP to execute invention assignment agreements in favor of the Company, in a form approved by the Management Committee, assigning all right, title, and interest in and to any Co-Developed IP to the Company.
